alt: Ode on a Distant Prospect of Eton College (1747)
content: Gray's Ode on a Distant Prospect of Eton College published anonymously by Robert Dodsley in 1747.
Gray completed the Elegy at Stoke Poges and sent it to Horace Walpole who circulated it in MS among his friends and acquaintances.

alt: Odes by Mr. Gray (The Progress of Poesy and The Bard) (1757)
content: Odes by Mr. Gray (The Progress of Poesy and The Bard) published by Horace Walpole at his new Strawberry Hill press in 1757.
Gray arrived back in England from the Grand Tour he undertook with Horace Walpole between 1739 and 1741.
Gray returned to Peterhouse, as a Fellow-Commoner, and resided permanently at Cambridge, with a few protracted absences, for the rest of his life.
Gray gave up his residence in London, where he had stayed since 1759 mainly to study at the newly opened British Museum, and returned to Pembroke College, Cambridge.
